system_recommendation.executive_summary: Hormuz crisis entering fourth month with gradual de-escalation signals. Brent declining to low $70s as diplomatic resolution progresses. Import-dependent economies should begin normalising Gulf crude nominations while maintaining diversified term contracts as a structural hedge against future corridor risks.

actual_outcome.documented_response: India resumed normal Gulf crude nominations as Hormuz corridor reopened under international naval escort protocol. MEA confirmed restored shipping schedules. PPAC reported crisis management cost of approximately $2.1B in additional freight and insurance premiums over the closure period.
